mapping.a_element: clause-to-variable ratio (constraint density) as the control parameter
mapping.b_element: the scaling of observations n relative to dimension p and sparsity k

mapping.a_element: sharp SAT/UNSAT phase transition with an intermediate phase separating easy from hard regions
mapping.b_element: threshold on n separating reliable from unreliable support recovery, with three measurement-sparsity regimes

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is the zero-temperature cavity method of spin-glass statistical mechanics, which yields a phase diagram, an order parameter (surveys of local magnetic fields), and a message-passing algorithm (survey propagation); Paper B's machinery is information-theoretic converse/achievability analysis (necessary and sufficient conditions) on the optimal decoder's fundamental limits, with no order parameter, no cavity/message-passing computation, and no algorithm. The only genuine overlap is the generic notion of a sharp threshold in a random high-dimensional problem, so the driving mechanisms differ despite the partial control-parameter/threshold correspondence.
